title: The Seventh Sunday after Pentecost
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
Sermon Date: July 7, 2024
Preacher: Katie Pearson

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

This is the Seventh Sunday after Pentecost, falling within Ordinary Time in the liturgical calendar. The Revised Common Lectionary readings for this date would have included 2 Samuel 5:1-5, 12 (David anointed king), Psalm 48, Ephesians 1:3-14 (spiritual blessings in Christ), and Mark 6:14-29 (the death of John the Baptist). The cathedral would have been observing the summer season of the church year, post-Pentecost and before Advent preparations.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in the immediate aftermath of its 81st General Convention, held in Louisville, Kentucky just two weeks prior (June 25-July 2), which had passed significant resolutions on gender-affirming healthcare and LGBTQ+ inclusion. The Diocese of Colorado, under Bishop Kimberly Lucas (elected 2023), was navigating post-General Convention implementation discussions. No major diocesan elections or crisis events were occurring at this specific moment.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was experiencing typical summer heat and activity in early July. The Fourth of July holiday had just passed on July 4th with typical Independence Day celebrations across the metro area. Saint John's Cathedral, located at 14th and Logan in downtown Denver, would have been part of conversations about downtown development and Capitol Hill neighborhood renewal initiatives that were ongoing throughout 2024. No major weather emergencies or crisis events were documented for this specific week.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

President Joe Biden had recently announced on July 5, 2024 (two days before this sermon) that he would not seek re-election, dramatically reshifting the presidential campaign landscape heading into the Democratic National Convention in Chicago. Vice President Kamala Harris was rapidly consolidating support to become the Democratic nominee. The U.S. Supreme Court had just concluded its term with multiple controversial decisions, including the Trump immunity ruling (late June) still dominating national discourse. The country was in a period of significant political upheaval and uncertainty about the November election.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

Russia's ongoing war in Ukraine remained in its second full year with significant fighting in the Donbas region and ongoing NATO involvement debates. The Middle East tensions persisted following the October 2023 Hamas attack and subsequent Israel-Gaza conflict, which continued to dominate international news. Global attention was turning toward the 2024 Paris Olympics, scheduled to begin in just one month (July 26), with preparations intensifying.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

America was in a state of political shock and recalibration following Biden's withdrawal from the race just 48 hours prior, creating a sense of uncertainty mixed with Democratic mobilization around Harris. The summer cultural mood was intense and divided, with election anxiety high despite traditional summer entertainment (upcoming Olympics, summer blockbuster movies). Denver, as a purple state urban center, was particularly engaged in election politics, and younger voters and LGBTQ+ communities were paying close attention to Harris's positioning on abortion rights and LGBTQ+ issues—topics the recent General Convention had just addressed.
